title: Implement Azure Blob Storage backend (BlobStore) for DemoStore
body:
## Description

Implement a new `BlobStore` class in `services/registry-api/src/store/` that implements the existing `DemoStore` interface, persisting demos and kiosk settings as JSON blobs in Azure Blob Storage.

### Storage Design
- Store demos as `demos.json` (array of `Demo` objects) in a single blob
- Store settings as `settings.json` (single `KioskSettings` object) in a single blob
- Use the existing storage account **`hubdemokioskst`** with a new blob container (e.g. `demo-registry`)

### Authentication
- Use `@azure/identity` `DefaultAzureCredential` for authentication — this automatically picks up the **System Assigned Managed Identity** when running in ACA
- **No connection strings or shared keys** — the storage account has `--allow-shared-key-access false`
- The `BlobServiceClient` must be constructed via `new BlobServiceClient(url, new DefaultAzureCredential())`

### Dependencies
- Add `@azure/storage-blob` and `@azure/identity` to `services/registry-api/package.json`

### Configuration via Environment Variables
- `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME` — storage account name (e.g. `hubdemokioskst`)
- `AZURE_STORAGE_CONTAINER_NAME` — blob container name (e.g. `demo-registry`)

### Implementation Notes
- Read-on-startup, write-on-mutate pattern (read full JSON blob, deserialize, mutate, serialize, write back)
- Use ETag-based optimistic concurrency on writes to prevent lost updates
- Handle `BlobNotFound` (404) gracefully on first read — treat as empty collection
- Keep `InMemoryStore` as-is for local development

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] New `BlobStore` class in `services/registry-api/src/store/blob-store.ts` implementing `DemoStore`
- [ ] Uses `@azure/identity` `DefaultAzureCredential` (System Assigned MI) — no keys or connection strings
- [ ] `demos.json` and `settings.json` blobs are read/written correctly
- [ ] ETag-based optimistic concurrency on blob writes
- [ ] Graceful handling of missing blobs on first startup (empty state)
- [ ] Exported from `services/registry-api/src/store/index.ts`
- [ ] Unit tests with mocked blob client verifying CRUD operations

title: Add environment-variable-driven store backend selection
body:
## Description

Update `services/registry-api/src/index.ts` to select the store backend based on an environment variable, allowing the API to switch between in-memory (local dev) and Azure Blob Storage (production) without code changes.

### Environment Variable
- `STORE_BACKEND` — accepted values: `memory` (default) | `blob`
- When `blob`, require `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME` and `AZURE_STORAGE_CONTAINER_NAME` to be set; fail fast with a clear error if missing

### Changes to `index.ts`
```
const store = STORE_BACKEND === 'blob'
  ? new BlobStore(accountName, containerName)
  : new InMemoryStore();
```

### Startup Logging
- Log which store backend is active: `Store backend: blob (account: hubdemokioskst, container: demo-registry)` or `Store backend: memory`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `STORE_BACKEND=memory` (or unset) uses `InMemoryStore` — no regression for local dev
- [ ] `STORE_BACKEND=blob` uses `BlobStore` with the configured account/container
- [ ] Missing `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME` or `AZURE_STORAGE_CONTAINER_NAME` when `STORE_BACKEND=blob` causes a clear startup error
- [ ] Store backend selection is logged at startup
- [ ] `.env.example` updated with new environment variables

title: Enable System Assigned MI on ca-registry-api & grant Storage Blob Data Contributor
body:
## Description

The `ca-registry-api` Container App needs a **System Assigned Managed Identity** with the **Storage Blob Data Contributor** role on the existing storage account `hubdemokioskst` to authenticate blob operations without keys.

### Current State
- `ca-admin` already has a system-assigned MI with `Storage Blob Data Contributor` for the Easy Auth token store (see bootstrap.sh Step 12.1)
- `ca-registry-api` does NOT have a system-assigned MI — only a user-assigned MI for ACR pull
- Storage account `hubdemokioskst` has `--allow-shared-key-access false` and `--default-action Deny`

### Required Changes in `bootstrap.sh`
1. After deploying `ca-registry-api` (Step 10), add a new step to:
   - Enable system-assigned MI: `az containerapp identity assign --name ca-registry-api --system-assigned`
   - Retrieve the principal ID
   - Assign `Storage Blob Data Contributor` role scoped to the `hubdemokioskst` storage account resource ID
2. Follow the same idempotent pattern used in Step 12.1 for `ca-admin` (check existing role assignment before creating)
3. Pass the following env vars to `ca-registry-api`:
   - `STORE_BACKEND=blob`
   - `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME=hubdemokioskst`
   - `AZURE_STORAGE_CONTAINER_NAME=demo-registry`

### Important Notes
- The storage account name is `hubdemokioskst` (user-provided, not the auto-derived name from bootstrap.sh)
- Role assignment propagation can take up to 10 minutes after creation — the readiness probe should account for this
- Both the user-assigned MI (ACR pull) and system-assigned MI (blob access) will coexist on the same container app

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ca-registry-api` has system-assigned managed identity enabled
- [ ] `Storage Blob Data Contributor` role assigned to `ca-registry-api` MI scoped to `hubdemokioskst`
- [ ] Role assignment is idempotent (safe to re-run bootstrap.sh)
- [ ] `STORE_BACKEND`, `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME`, `AZURE_STORAGE_CONTAINER_NAME` env vars set on `ca-registry-api`
- [ ] bootstrap.sh step follows existing patterns and is well-documented

title: Create demo-registry blob container in hubdemokioskst
body:
## Description

Provision a `demo-registry` blob container in the existing `hubdemokioskst` storage account for storing demo configuration and kiosk settings.

### Current State
- `hubdemokioskst` already exists and has the `easyauthtokens` blob container (created via ARM management plane in bootstrap.sh Step 6.1)
- The storage account has `--default-action Deny` and `--allow-shared-key-access false`, so container creation must use the **ARM management plane** (`az rest`) — NOT the data plane SDK

### Required Changes in `bootstrap.sh`
Add a new blob container creation step (following the same pattern as the `easyauthtokens` container in Step 6.1):

1. Derive the storage resource ID for `hubdemokioskst`
2. Use `az rest --method PUT` against the ARM API to create the `demo-registry` container
3. Handle already-exists (409) gracefully

**Note:** Since the user wants to use `hubdemokioskst` (a specific existing account) rather than the auto-derived storage account name, the bootstrap script needs to reference this account name. Consider making it configurable via an environment variable (e.g. `DEMO_STORAGE_ACCOUNT`) with `hubdemokioskst` as the default.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `demo-registry` blob container exists in `hubdemokioskst` after bootstrap
- [ ] Creation uses ARM management plane (`az rest`) to bypass data plane firewall
- [ ] Idempotent — re-running bootstrap doesn't fail if container already exists
- [ ] Storage account name is configurable (env var) for different environments

title: Configure network access from ACA environment to hubdemokioskst
body:
## Description

The `hubdemokioskst` storage account has `--default-action Deny` (no public network access). ACA container apps accessing blob storage via the data plane SDK are **not** covered by `--bypass AzureServices` (that bypass applies to specific trusted Azure services like Backup, not general compute). Network-level access must be explicitly configured.

### Options (pick one)

#### Option A: Add ACA outbound IPs to storage firewall (simplest)
- ACA environments have static outbound IPs
- Retrieve them via: `az containerapp env show --name <env> --query 'properties.staticIp'` or `properties.outboundIpAddresses`
- Add them to the storage account network rules: `az storage account network-rule add --ip-address <ip>`
- **Pros:** Simplest to implement, no VNet required
- **Cons:** Outbound IPs may change if the ACA environment is recreated; ties to specific environment

#### Option B: Private endpoint (most secure, recommended)
- Create a private endpoint for the `hubdemokioskst` storage account in the ACA environment's VNet
- Requires the ACA environment to be VNet-integrated (custom VNet)
- Create private DNS zone for `privatelink.blob.core.windows.net`
- **Pros:** Most secure, no public IP exposure, resilient to environment recreation
- **Cons:** Requires VNet-integrated ACA environment; more infrastructure to manage

#### Option C: Service endpoint on ACA subnet
- Enable Microsoft.Storage service endpoint on the ACA infrastructure subnet
- Add the subnet to storage account VNet rules
- **Pros:** Simpler than private endpoint, still VNet-bound
- **Cons:** Requires VNet-integrated ACA environment

### Recommendation
Start with **Option A** (outbound IPs) for simplicity and iterate to Option B if security requirements increase. Document the tradeoff.

### Required Changes
- Update `bootstrap.sh` to retrieve ACA environment outbound IPs and add them to `hubdemokioskst` firewall rules
- OR set up private endpoint infrastructure
- Ensure the network rule is in place before deploying `ca-registry-api` with `STORE_BACKEND=blob`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ca-registry-api` can reach `hubdemokioskst` blob endpoints from within ACA
- [ ] Network access method is documented in bootstrap.sh comments
- [ ] Idempotent — re-running bootstrap doesn't create duplicate rules
- [ ] Readiness probe confirms blob storage is reachable at startup

title: Update CD workflow to deploy registry-api with blob store env vars
body:
## Description

Update `.github/workflows/cd.yml` to pass the required environment variables for the Azure Blob Storage backend when deploying `ca-registry-api`.

### Current State
- The `cd.yml` deploy matrix for `registry-api` only sets `CORS_ORIGIN='*'` as an env var
- The YAML template generation in the "Generate deployment template" step conditionally adds env vars based on matrix fields

### Required Changes
Add the following env vars to the `registry-api` matrix entry and template generation:

```yaml
- app: registry-api
  ca-name: ca-registry-api
  target-port: 3001
  startup-path: /health/startup
  liveness-path: /health/live
  readiness-path: /health/ready
  cors-origin: "*"
  store-backend: blob
  storage-account-name: hubdemokioskst
  storage-container-name: demo-registry
```

Update the template generation step to include these as container env vars:
- `STORE_BACKEND`
- `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME`
- `AZURE_STORAGE_CONTAINER_NAME`

### Important Notes
- The storage account name and container name should ideally come from GitHub Actions variables (`vars.AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME`) rather than being hardcoded, for environment portability
- Ensure `CORS_ORIGIN` is still set alongside the new vars (don't accidentally drop it)
- The system-assigned MI must already be configured on the container app (done via bootstrap.sh) — the CD workflow doesn't need to manage identity

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `STORE_BACKEND=blob` env var set on `ca-registry-api` in CD deployment
- [ ] `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME` and `AZURE_STORAGE_CONTAINER_NAME` env vars set
- [ ] `CORS_ORIGIN='*'` is preserved (not accidentally removed)
- [ ] Values sourced from GitHub Actions variables where possible (not hardcoded)
- [ ] Existing launcher and admin deployments are unaffected

title: Implement seed data & first-run detection for blob store
body:
## Description

When the `BlobStore` starts for the first time against an empty blob container, it should automatically seed the default demos and settings so the kiosk is immediately usable.

### Behavior
1. On startup, `BlobStore` attempts to read `demos.json` from the blob container
2. If the blob does not exist (HTTP 404 / `BlobNotFound`), write the seed demos (from `SEED_DEMOS` constant) to `demos.json`
3. Similarly, if `settings.json` doesn't exist, write `DEFAULT_SETTINGS`
4. If blobs already exist (even if empty array `[]`), do NOT overwrite — respect user changes
5. Log seed actions: `Seeded N default demos to blob storage` or `Loaded N demos from blob storage`

### Seed Data Source
- Extract `SEED_DEMOS` and `DEFAULT_SETTINGS` from `in-memory.ts` into a shared `seed-data.ts` file
- Both `InMemoryStore` and `BlobStore` import from this shared source
- This ensures consistency and avoids duplication

### Edge Cases
- Race condition: if two replicas start simultaneously and both detect empty blobs, only one write should succeed (use ETag / If-None-Match on the initial write)
- If blob read fails for reasons other than 404 (e.g., network error, auth failure), fail startup rather than overwriting production data with seeds

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Empty blob container is auto-seeded with default demos and settings on first startup
- [ ] Existing data in blobs is never overwritten by seed data
- [ ] Seed data is shared between `InMemoryStore` and `BlobStore` (no duplication)
- [ ] Race condition handled via ETag / If-None-Match on seed writes
- [ ] Clear startup logging distinguishes seeded vs. loaded state
- [ ] Non-404 blob errors cause startup failure (don't silently seed over production data)

title: Update readiness probe to verify blob storage connectivity
body:
## Description

The existing `/health/ready` endpoint should verify that the blob storage backend is reachable and the managed identity credentials are valid, so ACA can accurately report readiness.

### Current State
- `services/registry-api/src/routes/health.ts` has `/health/startup`, `/health/live`, and `/health/ready` endpoints
- Readiness is currently a simple boolean flag set at startup

### Required Changes
1. When `STORE_BACKEND=blob`, the readiness probe should perform a lightweight blob operation (e.g., check container existence or get container properties) to verify:
   - Network connectivity to the storage account
   - Managed identity token acquisition works
   - The blob container exists
2. Cache the result for a short TTL (e.g., 30s) to avoid hitting storage on every probe interval
3. If the check fails, return HTTP 503 (not ready) so ACA stops routing traffic
4. Liveness probe (`/health/live`) should remain lightweight — do NOT add blob checks there (to avoid restart loops if storage is temporarily down)
5. When `STORE_BACKEND=memory`, readiness behaves as it does today (always ready after startup)

### Important: Role Propagation Delay
- After bootstrap.sh assigns the `Storage Blob Data Contributor` role, propagation can take up to 10 minutes
- The startup probe should have a generous timeout/retry to accommodate this
- Consider a startup initialization loop that retries blob access with backoff before marking ready

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `/health/ready` verifies blob storage connectivity when `STORE_BACKEND=blob`
- [ ] Readiness check result is cached (30s TTL) to reduce storage calls
- [ ] Returns 503 if blob storage is unreachable or MI auth fails
- [ ] `/health/live` is NOT affected (stays lightweight)
- [ ] Handles role propagation delay gracefully at startup (retry with backoff)
- [ ] No change in behavior when `STORE_BACKEND=memory`

title: Add Azurite support in docker-compose for local blob store testing
body:
## Description

Add an [Azurite](https://github.com/Azure/Azurite) service to `docker-compose.yml` so developers can test the `BlobStore` backend locally without an Azure subscription.

### Changes to `docker-compose.yml`
1. Add Azurite service using the official `mcr.microsoft.com/azure-storage/azurite` image
2. Expose blob service port (10000)
3. Add a `docker-compose.blob.yml` override (or profile) that configures `registry-api` with:
   - `STORE_BACKEND=blob`
   - `AZURE_STORAGE_ACCOUNT_NAME=devstoreaccount1` (Azurite default)
   - `AZURE_STORAGE_CONTAINER_NAME=demo-registry`
   - `AZURITE_ENABLED=true` (or `AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING` pointing to Azurite)

### Authentication Note
- Azurite uses the well-known dev storage credentials, not managed identity
- The `BlobStore` constructor should support an optional connection string override for local dev: if `AZURE_STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING` is set, use `BlobServiceClient.fromConnectionString()` instead of `DefaultAzureCredential`
- This keeps production secure (MI only) while enabling local testing

### Documentation
- Update `README.md` with instructions for running with blob storage locally:
  - `docker compose --profile blob up` (or `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.blob.yml up`)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Azurite service added to docker-compose (as a profile or override file)
- [ ] `registry-api` can use `BlobStore` against local Azurite
- [ ] `BlobStore` supports connection string fallback for Azurite (local dev only)
- [ ] Default `docker compose up` still uses `InMemoryStore` (no breaking change)
- [ ] README documents local blob storage testing workflow
